docs.title: Welcome to MkDocs
docs.text: For full documentation visit mkdocs.org.

docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
• mkdocs new [dir-name] - Create a new project.
  • mkdocs serve - Start the live-reloading docs server.
  • mkdocs build - Build the documentation site.
  • mkdocs -h - Print help message and exit.

docs.title: Project layout
docs.text:
mkdocs.yml    # The configuration file.
docs/
    index.md  # The documentation homepage.
    ...       # Other markdown pages, images and other files.

docs.title: Public API
docs.text:
keydaemon — background daemon for keyboard/mouse automation.

Public API

macro() → MacroBuilder (fluent builder) preset(name) → MacroBuilder (pre-configured) load(name) → MacroBuilder (from TOML file) stop_all() → None

docs.title: macro()
docs.text: Return a new empty MacroBuilder.

docs.title: preset(name)
docs.text:
Load a named built-in preset and return its pre-configured MacroBuilder.

Available presets: minecraft_afk

Raises:

Type Description ImportError

if no preset with that name exists.

docs.title: load(name)
docs.text:
Load a macro or profile from a TOML file in the keydaemon data directory.

For profiles, returns a builder whose .run() starts all listed macros.

docs.title: stop_all()
docs.text:
Kill everything. Two independent passes so nothing can survive:

  1. Ordered teardown through the profile tree — stops runners the clean way (releases held inputs, stops listeners child-first).
  2. Flat backstop sweep of every runner ever created — catches anything the tree can't reach (e.g. a child whose owning profile was garbage collected, since _PROFILE_REGISTRY holds only weak references).

Runner.stop() is idempotent, so a runner hit by both passes stops once.

docs.title: MacroBuilder
docs.text: Fluent builder — accumulates state, does nothing until .run().

docs.title: drag_to(x1, y1, x2, y2)
docs.text: Sugar: move to start, hold left, smooth move to end, release.

docs.title: hotkey(key, mode='toggle')
docs.text:
Arm this macro behind a hotkey instead of running immediately.

mode="toggle" (default): press the key to start the loop, press again to stop it. mode="once": each press fires the loop a single time. The program stays alive between presses; use exit_key to quit entirely.

docs.title: times_per_second(n)
docs.text:
Set the loop rate as a frequency instead of a period.

times_per_second(20) is sugar for every(1 / 20). Clickers and anti-AFK loops are easier to reason about in Hz than in seconds.

docs.title: InputController
docs.text:
Bases: Protocol

Structural protocol — tests inject a mock without subclassing.

docs.title: SelfStopAction dataclass
docs.text: Signals the owning runner to stop via its UUID token.

docs.title: Scheduler
docs.text:
Blocking scheduler loop — intended to run inside a DaemonRunner thread.

Executes the action list, waits the interval (with jitter), then repeats. Sleeps in SCHEDULER_TICK increments so stop_requested() is checked frequently.

docs.title: color_matches(x, y, hex_color, tolerance=10)
docs.text: Return True if the pixel at (x, y) is within tolerance of hex_color.

docs.title: get_pixel_color(x, y)
docs.text: Return the RGB color of the pixel at screen coordinates (x, y).

docs.title: get_pixel_hex(x, y)
docs.text: Return the hex color string of the pixel at (x, y), e.g. '#3A7D44'.

docs.title: hex_to_rgb(hex_color)
docs.text: Convert a hex color string like '#3A7D44' or '3A7D44' to (r, g, b).

docs.title: Code examples
docs.text: An example of a codeblock for Python:

docs.title: Code Blocks
docs.text:
Python CodeThe code again add_numbers.py
# Function to add two numbers
def add_two_numbers(num1, num2):
    return num1 + num2

# Example usage
result = add_two_numbers(5, 3)
print('The sum is:', result)
add_numbers.py
# Function to add two numbers
def add_two_numbers(num1, num2):
    return num1 + num2

# Example usage
result = add_two_numbers(5, 3)
print('The sum is:', result)

This is amazing

Where are we?

This is hidden

Where are we?

docs.title: Flowchart
docs.text:
graph LR
  A[Start] --> B{Failure?};
  B -->|Yes| C[Investigate...];
  C --> D[Debug];
  D --> B;
  B ---->|No| E[Success!];

docs.title: Sequence Diagrams
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  Server->>Terminal: Send request
  loop Health
      Terminal->>Terminal: Check for health
  end
  Note right of Terminal: System online
  Terminal-->>Server: Everything is OK
  Terminal->>Database: Request customer data
  Database-->>Terminal: Customer data
classDiagram
  Person <|-- Student
  Person <|-- Professor
  Person : +String name
  Person : +String phoneNumber
  Person : +String emailAddress
  Person: +purchaseParkingPass()
  Address "1" <-- "0..1" Person:lives at
  class Student{
    +int studentNumber
    +int averageMark
    +isEligibleToEnrol()
    +getSeminarsTaken()
  }
  class Professor{
    +int salary
  }
  class Address{
    +String street
    +String city
    +String state
    +int postalCode
    +String country
    -validate()
    +outputAsLabel()
  }
